items.q: How do you say 'I get up' using a reflexive verb?
items.why: Students drop the reflexive pronoun; daily-routine verbs like 'se lever' need 'me/te/se'.

items.q: Which auxiliary do reflexive verbs take in the perfect tense?
items.why: Students use 'avoir'; reflexive verbs form the passé composé with 'être' and the participle agrees.

items.q: How do you say 'half past seven'?
items.why: Students confuse time phrases; 'et demie' is half past, 'et quart' is quarter past.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get up at seven o'clock'?
items.correct: Je me lève à sept heures.
items.why: Correct answer: Je me lève à sept heures.. “Je lève à sept heures.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: 'Se brosser les dents' is an example of which type of verb?
items.why: Correct answer: A reflexive (pronominal) verb. “An adjective” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Breakfast. “Dinner” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
